root_text.iti71:2.1: “Diṭṭhā mayā, bhikkhave, sattā kāyasucaritena samannāgatā vacīsucaritena samannāgatā manosucaritena samannāgatā ariyānaṁ anupavādakā sammādiṭṭhikā sammādiṭṭhikammasamādānā.
root_text.iti71:2.2: Te kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapannā.
root_text.iti71:3.1: Taṁ kho panāhaṁ, bhikkhave, nāññassa samaṇassa vā brāhmaṇassa vā sutvā vadāmi.
root_text.iti71:3.2: Diṭṭhā mayā, bhikkhave, sattā kāyasucaritena samannāgatā vacīsucaritena samannāgatā manosucaritena samannāgatā ariyānaṁ anupavādakā sammādiṭṭhikā sammādiṭṭhikammasamādānā.
root_text.iti71:3.3: Te kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapannā.
root_text.iti71:3.4: Api ca, bhikkhave, yadeva sāmaṁ ñātaṁ sāmaṁ diṭṭhaṁ sāmaṁ viditaṁ tadevāhaṁ vadāmi.
root_text.iti71:4.1: Diṭṭhā mayā, bhikkhave, sattā kāyasucaritena samannāgatā vacīsucaritena samannāgatā manosucaritena samannāgatā ariyānaṁ anupavādakā sammādiṭṭhikā sammādiṭṭhikammasamādānā.
root_text.iti71:4.2: Te kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapannā”ti.
root_text.iti71:7.1: Ayampi attho vutto bhagavatā, iti me sutanti.

translation_text.iti71:1.1: This was said by the Buddha, the Perfected One: that is what I heard.
translation_text.iti71:2.1: “Mendicants, I’ve seen beings who engaged in good conduct of body, speech, and mind, who did not denounce the noble ones, who held right view and acted accordingly.
translation_text.iti71:2.2: At the breaking up of the body, after death, they were reborn in a good destination, a heavenly realm.
translation_text.iti71:3.1: Now, I don’t say this because I’ve heard it from some other ascetic or brahmin.
translation_text.iti71:3.4: I only say it because I’ve known, seen, and realized it for myself.”
translation_text.iti71:5.1: “When the mind has been directed right,
translation_text.iti71:5.3: and right bodily deeds have been done,
translation_text.iti71:6.2: though their life may be short,
translation_text.iti71:6.3: when their body breaks up, that wise person
translation_text.iti71:7.1: This too is a matter that was spoken by the Blessed One: that is what I heard.

variant_text.iti71:5.2: sammā vācañca bhāsiya → sammā vācaṁ abhāsiya (si, sya-all, pts-vp-pli1)
